title: Sambal Green Bean Omelette with Rice
summary: This is a recreation of one of Xien's favourite childhood dishes – Chinese homestyle green bean omelette served with rice, and Mama Yu’s Sambal, of course. It’s a simple dish but when seasoned right, the flavours really come out, making it the perfect quick and easy weeknight meal.

method.description: Wash your rice three times until the water runs clear. Drain, then transfer to a large saucepan and add water until it reaches just below your first knuckle. Bring to the boil over a medium-high heat, then turn the heat to low and cover with a lid for 12 mins.
Meanwhile, mince the garlic. Wash the green beans, chop off the ends then finely chop into small pieces.
Whisk the eggs in a large bowl and season with the salt, white pepper and curry powder.

Add a glug of oil to a wok over a medium-high heat and add the minced garlic. Fry until lightly golden, then add chopped green beans and sauté for 2-3 mins. Remove and add everything to the bowl of whisked eggs.
Add a small ladle of oil to the now-empty wok, then add the eggs and green beans mixture back into the wok.
Fry the eggs for 1-2 mins on one side, then flip and fry for 1-2 mins, depending on how cooked you like your omelette.
Serve the omelette with rice, sambal and crispy shallots on top. Enjoy!

chefs.name: Xiengni Zhou
chefs.summary: As a passionate home cook and co-founder of Mama Yu's Chilli Oil, Xiengni (@xiensscran) is all about flavour and always cooked from the heart. Sharing three generations of recipes, her approach to food and family and what means is a breath of fresh air.
